free_text: Almost the same Table as in
Mean kinetic energies and angular distributions
of fission fragments from Pu-238 and Pu-239
Direct counting of fission fragments using
1. The double target 11 mm in diameter had 150 mu-g of
Pu-238 on one side and the same amount of Pu-239 on
the other. Pu-238 targets contained 14.4% Pu-239.
Samples contained 40 mu-g/cm2 of each isotope.
1. The 10 x 18 mm detectors were placed on a circle of
radius 52 mm, eight for each isotope. In front of the
ring of detectors was a ring bearing 1.5 mg/cm2 Al
filters, which do not absorb the fragments. The entire
system was enclosed in a cadmium case.
2. detectors with 1.85 mg/cm2 Al filters, which were
sensitive to the shape of the fragment mass
distribution. Each glass detector had a space free
from filters that was used to monitor the etching .
Detector efficiency was calibrated by fragments from
The coefficient of angular anisotropy of fission
fragments is presented only for Pu-238
